metadata.title:
CALLS EXPERT REPORT


LABYRINTH OF MYSTERY

pages.raw_text:
However, he pleads guilty to
violating the city charter. We have
wondered how this city has been
kept on cash basis. He has vio-
lated the charter to do it, and has
saved the city thousands of dollars
in interest. Since the panic of .Octo.
Der, 1907, he saved the city $357.65
interest on crematory warrants;
$1,222.01 interest on bridge war
rants and $2,42779 interest on
water warrants, total of $4,007.45.
1t is a pity this city has not more
officers like him.


"The whole exporting has been
shrouded in mystery, and is stig-
matism of the treasurer. 1t is I'M
demanded chicanery chasing the
treasurer through a labyrinth of
mystery"


Councilman Mohr took the part
of the experts by sarcastically re-
marking that they were not to
blame for not reporting direct to
the council because they had been
hired by an individual to whom
they reported Mayor Moore was
the direct agent in their hiring,
but Mohr wouldn't state who hired
them.


Eggleston's saving of interest
was effected by close financiering
carrying the city on a cash basis
in his own Office instead of follow
ing the warrant custom.
